_comment: Per-integration CEL rules for process auto-discovery, evaluated by analysis/scripts/process_cel_eval.py. Format mirrors `CELSelector.Processes` in the agent's auto-discovery config (comp/core/autodiscovery/integration). Fields available in CEL: process.name (from /proc/<pid>/status Name, 15 char kernel limit), process.cmdline (joined cmdline), process.args (list). When an integration is omitted here the script falls back to manifest.json process_signatures, OR-joined as cmdline.contains(<sig>). Use this file to add rules for integrations whose manifest lacks signatures or whose signatures are insufficient/wrong.

squid.notes: Targets the squid daemon (cmdline 'squid -f /etc/squid/squid.conf'). The log-tailing helpers 'tail -F .../access.log' do not match.

nifi.notes: Targets the NiFi JVM. Disco labels helpers (tail) and the python framework subprocess as separate generated_names; those don't match.

riak.notes: Targets the Erlang beam.smp running Riak. The 'tail -n 1024 -f /var/log/riak/console.log' helper doesn't match.

scylla.notes: Targets the scylla daemon. The container-side supervisord, rsyslogd and node_exporter don't match.

silverstripe_cms.notes: silverstripe_cms is an Apache/PHP CMS check; target the apache2 process.

tls.notes: tls test fixture runs nginx, mysqld and postgres for TLS testing. The integration would target nginx.

clickhouse.notes: Targets clickhouse-server. Multi-node test env yields N>1 survivors.

hazelcast.notes: Hazelcast JVM with explicit main class.

kafka_consumer.notes: Kafka broker JVM main class. Excludes zookeeper JVM in same env.

confluent_platform.notes: Confluent stack: kafka brokers + zookeeper + various io.confluent.* services.

prefect.expression: process.cmdline.contains('prefect server start') || process.cmdline.contains('prefect worker start')

elastic.notes: Manifest signature 'java org.elasticsearch...' does not match real cmdline (which is '/path/to/java ... org.elasticsearch...'). Match the main class only. Test fixture runs OpenSearch.

pulsar.notes: Manifest signature 'java org.apache.pulsar...' assumes cmdline starts with 'java '. Real cmdline has the full java path.

spark.notes: Manifest signature 'java org.apache.spark...' has same issue as elastic/pulsar — drop the 'java ' prefix.

sonarqube.notes: Same as elastic. Manifest signature 'java org.sonar...' won't match real cmdline.

hivemq.notes: HiveMQ JVM. Need to match path/jar since main class may not appear in cmdline.

quarkus.notes: Quarkus run jar. Real cmdline has '/path/to/java ... -jar quarkus-run.jar'; the 'java ' prefix in the manifest signature breaks matching.

zk.notes: ZooKeeper. Match the main class or config file rather than the 'java ' prefix.

teamcity.notes: TeamCity JVM. Manifest signature 'teamcity-server.sh' assumes cmdline matches the launcher shell script, not the actual JVM cmdline.

powerdns_recursor.notes: PowerDNS recursor — the manifest signature targets pdns_server (authoritative server), not the recursor binary used by this integration.

couchbase.notes: Manifest signature 'beam.smp couchbase' does not appear literally in any cmdline. Match the couchbase erlang lib path. Couchbase runs 3 beam.smp roles (babysitter, ns_server, ns_couchdb); a precise rule would target the babysitter only.

marathon.notes: Marathon JVM main class. Manifest signature 'start --master mesos marathon' doesn't appear in the actual cmdline.

temporal.notes: Temporal Go binary. The collected data for this integration unfortunately did not include the temporal-server process (sibling container healthcheck failure during collection).
